app_recommendation: Discuss endocrine therapy suitability; check menopausal status and contraindications.
scientific_rationale: Hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer is commonly managed with adjuvant endocrine therapy; extended AI therapy may be offered in node-positive disease.
source: ASCO adjuvant endocrine therapy guideline update: https://ascopubs.org/doi/10.1200/JCO.18.01160

trigger: HER2_STATUS is Positive or predicted subtype is Her2
app_recommendation: Confirm HER2 with pathology/IHC/FISH; discuss anti-HER2 regimen if clinically positive.
scientific_rationale: HER2-directed treatment depends on accurate HER2 testing; ASCO/CAP guidance emphasizes HER2 testing interpretation and IHC/ISH workflow.

app_recommendation: Discuss chemotherapy, immunotherapy eligibility, and BRCA/genetic testing where appropriate.
scientific_rationale: TNBC treatment commonly includes surgery/chemotherapy; immunotherapy such as pembrolizumab may be used with chemotherapy in selected settings.

app_recommendation: Review adjuvant escalation, closer follow-up, and clinical trial eligibility.
scientific_rationale: High predicted risk is not itself a guideline indication, but it is a prompt for multidisciplinary review and closer risk discussion.
source: Model-derived decision-support rule plus standard oncology risk-review practice.

app_recommendation: Verify nodal stage and discuss systemic therapy/radiotherapy implications.
scientific_rationale: Nodal involvement is a major prognostic and treatment-planning factor in breast cancer.
source: Clinical oncology staging/treatment-planning principle; check local guidelines.

app_recommendation: Treat as high-risk clinical feature in treatment planning.
scientific_rationale: Higher grade is associated with more aggressive biology and is commonly considered in adjuvant treatment decisions.
ASCO follow-up guidance recommends history/physical examination and mammography after primary treatment.

app_recommendation: Consider closer risk review and clinician-led prevention/screening discussion.
scientific_rationale: BCSC is a risk-stratification model, not a diagnosis model; high-risk groups may support screening/prevention discussion.
